pattern: This is a straight left-to-right simulation with one early-exit rule -- a running score and counter are updated event by event, and processing simply halts the moment the counter would hit its cap.
hints: Walk the events left to right, keeping a running score and a running counter, and check the stopping condition before handling each event (not after).
Only the literal token "W" ever touches the counter; every other recognized token adds to the score instead (a numeric digit adds its face value, and "WD"/"NB" both simply add 1).
Stop the moment the counter would reach 10 -- any events after that point are never processed, no matter what they are.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, counter caps at ten and stops
exactly nine W events, counter does not stop processing
counter reaches ten mid-array with more events queued behind it

stress.description: random tokens from the full allowed vocabulary
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    vocab = ['0', '1', '2', '3', '4', '6', 'W', 'WD', 'NB']
    events = [rng.choice(vocab) for _ in range(n)]
    return [events]
